title: Introduction
text:
The core of the Solow model relies on the Cobb-Douglas production function with constant returns to scale.1 We represent total output \(Y\) as a function of capital \(K\), labor \(L\), and the level of technology \(A\):
1 We can work with decreasing returns. Increasing returns are hard.\[
Y = F(K, AL) = K^\alpha (AL)^{1-\alpha}
\]
In per-capita terms, where \(y = Y/AL\) and \(k = K/AL\), the production function simplifies to:






Note 1: Production



Production of GDP per capita is determined as follows: \[
y = f(k) = k^\alpha
\]


This is a span that has the class column-margin which places it in the margin without the sidenote mark.

title: Introduction
text:
Simulating the Production Function
Below is a simple Python snippet to visualize how output \(y\) responds to changes in capital intensity \(k\) for different values of the capital share \(\alpha\). It uses the production function in Note 1

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Define the production function
def production_function(k, alpha):
    return k**alpha

# Parameters
k_values = np.linspace(0, 5, 100)
alphas = [0.3, 0.5, 0.7]

# Plotting
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))
for a in alphas:
    plt.plot(k_values, production_function(k_values, a), label=rf"$\alpha = {a}$")

plt.title("Diminishing Marginal Returns to Capital")
plt.xlabel("Capital per effective worker ($k$)")
plt.ylabel("Output per effective worker ($y$)")
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.show()







Figure 1: Output per effective worker as a function of capital intensity.

title: Macroeconomic Theory
text: This repository hosts the quarto docs, code, etc. for simulating various macroeconomic models.

2 Code
Pages contain code to run, and/or data you can manipulate.

title: Macroeconomic Theory
text:
3 Installation
The site is created using Quarto. You can use the site as-is. These notes are for me and how I set up the environment to use Quarto to prepare it.

Download the Quarto CLI
Create a virtual environment within the folder “python3 -m venv .venv”
Install Quarto Live using the command line “quarto add r-wasm/quarto-live”
